items.title: Scaling and normalization order
items.description: Numeric pipelines usually impute before scaling: standardizers need finite values, and imputation statistics must be fit on training data the same way scaler parameters are.

items.title: Native NaN handling in gradient boosting
items.description: XGBoost and LightGBM learn default split directions for missing values during training, offering an alternative to mean imputation when gaps are frequent and structurally informative.

items.title: Cleaning and transformation pipelines
items.description: Production data pipelines chain missing-value audit, imputation, encoding, and scaling as reproducible steps—often wrapped in sklearn Pipeline or workflow tools with versioned artifacts.

items.title: Leakage from global imputation
items.description: Computing fill values on train+validation+test before splitting leaks holdout distributions into preprocessing and inflates offline metrics—the same failure mode as global scaling.
